records.ety: [LL. minuta a small portion, small coin, fr. L. minutus small: cf. F. minute. See 4th Minute.]

defs: The sixtieth part of an hour; sixty seconds. (Abbrev. m. or min.; as, 4 h. 30 m.)
quotes: Four minutes, that is to say, minutes of an hour.

defs: The sixtieth part of a degree; sixty seconds (Marked thus (); as, 10° 20).
A nautical or a geographic mile.
A very small part of anything, or anything very small; a jot; a tittle.
quotes: Minutes and circumstances of his passion.

defs: A point of time; a moment.
quotes: I go this minute to attend the king.

defs: The memorandum; a record; a note to preserve the memory of anything; as, to take minutes of a contract; to take minutes of a conversation or debate; to read the minutes of the last meeting.
A fixed part of a module. See Module.
notes: ☞ Different writers take as the minute one twelfth, one eighteenth, one thirtieth, or one sixtieth part of the module.

defs: Of or pertaining to a minute or minutes; occurring at or marking successive minutes.

records.ety: [imp. & p. p. Minuted; p. pr. & vb. n. Minuting.]

defs: To set down a short sketch or note of; to jot down; to make a minute or a brief summary of.
quotes: The Empress of Russia, with her own hand, minuted an edict for universal tolerance.

records.ety: [L. minutus, p. p. of minuere to lessen. See Minish, Minor, and cf. Menu, Minuet.]
usage: -- Minute, Circumstantial, Particular. A circumstantial account embraces all the leading events; a particular account includes each event and movement, though of but little importance; a minute account goes further still, and omits nothing as to person, time, place, adjuncts, etc.

defs: Very small; little; tiny; fine; slight; slender; inconsiderable; as, minute details.
Attentive to small things; paying attention to details; critical; particular; precise; as, a minute observer; minute observation.
